# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

## Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. [KTU University Exam — May 2023, Model Paper]
**With a neat block diagram, explain the internal structure of IC 555 timer.**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
The IC 555 timer consists of the following internal blocks:
1. **Voltage Divider:** A chain of three equal 5 kΩ resistors between Vcc and GND, providing reference voltages of **(1/3)Vcc** and **(2/3)Vcc**.
2. **Two Comparators:** Comparator 1 compares the THRESHOLD pin with **(2/3)Vcc**, and Comparator 2 compares the TRIGGER pin with **(1/3)Vcc**.
3. **SR Flip-Flop:** Latches the comparator outputs to drive the output buffer and discharge transistor.
4. **Discharge Transistor (Pin 7):** An open-collector NPN that provides a discharge path for the timing capacitor.
5. **Output Buffer:** Provides a totem-pole drive capable of sourcing/sinking 200 mA.

---

### Q2. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2022]
**What is the difference between astable and monostable multivibrator? Compare their outputs.**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

| Parameter | Astable | Monostable |
|-----------|---------|------------|
| Stable states | **Zero** (no stable state) | **One** stable state, one quasi-stable |
| External trigger | Not required | Required |
| Output waveform | Continuous square wave | Single pulse per trigger |
| Application | Clock generators, PWM | Time-delay circuits, debouncers |
| IC 555 formula for time | T = 0.693(R1+2R2)C | T = 1.1 RC |

---

## Part B — 14-Mark Module Internal Choice Questions

### ⭐ Question A (14 Marks) — Astable Focus

**[KTU University Exam — Jul 2024, Supplied Model Paper]**

**(a)** With a neat circuit diagram, explain the operation of **IC 555 in astable mode**. Derive the expression for the **frequency of oscillation** and the **duty cycle**. (7 Marks)

**(b)** Design a 555 astable multivibrator to generate a **2 kHz** square wave with **75% duty cycle** using a **0.05 µF** capacitor. Verify your design by calculating t_HIGH, t_LOW, and the actual duty cycle. (7 Marks)

#### Model Solution (a):

**Circuit Description (3 marks):**
The 555 astable connection is: Pin 8 to Vcc, Pin 1 to GND, Pin 4 to Vcc (Reset disabled), Pin 5 to GND via 0.01 µF bypass. **R1** connects Vcc to Pin 7, **R2** connects Pin 7 to a common node, Pin 2 (Trigger) and Pin 6 (Threshold) are tied together at that common node. **C** connects the common node to GND. Output is taken from Pin 3.

**Operation (2 marks):** [See Section 2.2 for full Phase 1 / Phase 2 description.]

**Derivation (2 marks):** [Refer to Section 3.1, steps 1–9 above for the complete derivation leading to:

$$f = \frac{1.44}{(R_1 + 2R_2) C} \quad \text{and} \quad D = \frac{R_1 + R_2}{R_1 + 2R_2} \times 100\%
$$]

#### Model Solution (b):

**Given:** f = 2000 Hz, D = 0.75, C = 0.05 × 10⁻⁶ F = 50 nF.

**Step 1 — Calculate (R1 + 2R2).**

$$R_1 + 2 R_2 = \frac{1.44}{f \cdot C} = \frac{1.44}{2000 \times 50 \times 10^{-9}} = \frac{1.44}{10^{-4}} = 14{,}400 \, \Omega = 14.4 \, k\Omega$$

**Step 2 — Use duty cycle equation.**

$$0.75 = \frac{R_1 + R_2}{R_1 + 2 R_2} \quad \Rightarrow \quad 0.75 (R_1 + 2R_2) = R_1 + R_2$$
$$0.75 R_1 + 1.5 R_2 = R_1 + R_2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 0.5 R_2 = 0.25 R_1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad R_1 = 2 R_2$$

**Step 3 — Solve simultaneously.**

$$2 R_2 + 2 R_2 = 14.4 \, k\Omega \quad \Rightarrow \quad 4 R_2 = 14.4 \, k\Omega \quad \Rightarrow \quad R_2 = 3.6 \, k\Omega$$
$$R_1 = 7.2 \, k\Omega$$

**Step 4 — Choose standard E12 values:** R1 ≈ 6.8 kΩ + 390 Ω (or use 10 kΩ potentiometer), R2 ≈ 3.6 kΩ (or 3.3 kΩ + 300 Ω).

**Step 5 — Verification using standard R1 = 6.8 kΩ, R2 = 3.6 kΩ.**

$$T = 0.693 \times (6.8k + 2 \times 3.6k) \times 50n = 0.693 \times 14k \times 50n = 485 \, \mu s$$
$$f = 1/T = 2062 \, Hz \approx 2.06 \, kHz \checkmark$$
$$t_{HIGH} = 0.693 \times (6.8k + 3.6k) \times 50n = 0.693 \times 10.4k \times 50n = 360 \, \mu s$$
$$t_{LOW} = 0.693 \times 3.6k \times 50n = 124.7 \, \mu s$$
$$D = \frac{360}{485} \times 100\% = 74.2\% \approx 75\% \checkmark$$

---

### ⭐ Question B (14 Marks) — Monostable Focus

**[KTU University Exam — May 2024]**

**(a)** Draw the **circuit diagram of IC 555 in monostable mode** and explain its operation. Derive the expression for the output pulse width. (7 Marks)

**(b)** A 555 monostable is required to produce an output pulse of **1.5 ms**. If a **0.1 µF** capacitor is used, calculate the value of the timing resistor. Also explain what happens if the trigger pulse width is greater than the output pulse width — does the circuit malfunction? (7 Marks)

#### Model Solution (a):

**Circuit (2 marks):** Pin 8 to Vcc, Pin 1 to GND, Pin 4 to Vcc. **R** between Vcc and the common node (Pin 6 and Pin 7 tied). **C** between that common node and GND. Pin 2 (Trigger) connected to the input trigger source via a differentiating RC network (typically 10 kΩ + 0.01 µF) to make it edge-sensitive.

**Stable State (2 marks):** Discharge transistor ON → capacitor voltage = 0V → output LOW.

**Triggered State (2 marks):** Negative trigger on Pin 2 < Vcc/3 → flip-flop sets → output goes HIGH → discharge OFF → C charges from 0V toward Vcc through R. When Vc reaches 2Vcc/3, the threshold comparator resets the flip-flop → output goes LOW → discharge ON → C rapidly discharges → circuit returns to stable state.

**Derivation (1 mark):** [Refer to Section 3.2 — final result:

$$T = 1.1 \, R C
$$]

#### Model Solution (b):

**Step 1 — Calculate R for T = 1.5 ms with C = 0.1 µF.**

$$T = 1.1 \, R C \quad \Rightarrow \quad R = \frac{T}{1.1 \, C} = \frac{1.5 \times 10^{-3}}{1.1 \times 0.1 \times 10^{-6}} = \frac{1.5 \times 10^{-3}}{1.1 \times 10^{-7}}$$

$$R = 13{,}636 \, \Omega \approx 13.6 \, k\Omega \text{ (use 12 kΩ standard with 5 kΩ trim)} \checkmark$$

**Step 2 — Effect of long trigger pulse (2 marks):**
The 555 monostable is **level-sensitive** internally, but the output pulse width is determined solely by the **RC time constant** of the timing network — not by the trigger pulse width. Once the internal flip-flop is set by the negative edge of the trigger, the output remains HIGH for exactly **T = 1.1 RC**, regardless of how long the trigger pulse stays low. The output cannot be re-triggered during the timing cycle (this is called **non-retriggerable mode**). If a second trigger arrives during the quasi-stable state, it is ignored. Thus, the circuit **does not malfunction** for trigger pulses longer than T, as long as the trigger returns HIGH before the next required trigger event.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls (Lose 2–3 marks here):**
> 1. **Confusing the discharge path in astable mode:** Many students wrongly write that the capacitor discharges through **R1 + R2**. It discharges through **R2 only** because Pin 7 shorts directly to ground when the discharge transistor is ON. Examiners specifically test this.
> 2. **Forgetting the 0.693 factor:** The natural log of 2 is 0.693. Skipping this and writing "T = (R1+2R2)C" is a common error that yields wrong units interpretation.
> 3. **Mixing up 555 monostable and astable pin connections:** In monostable, Pin 6 and Pin 7 are **tied together** and R is connected to Vcc, NOT Pin 7 to Vcc. In astable, R1 connects Vcc to Pin 7, with R2 in series.
> 4. **Ignoring output current limits:** The 555 can only source/sink ~200 mA. Driving a low-impedance load (e.g., a relay coil) directly damages the IC. Always use a driver transistor.
> 5. **Not mentioning Pin 4 must be tied HIGH:** If Pin 4 (Reset) is floating, the IC randomly resets — this is a frequently asked troubleshooting question in the KTU viva.

---

## 📌 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

- ✅ **555 Astable Frequency:** $f = 1.44 / [(R_1 + 2R_2) C]$
- ✅ **555 Astable Duty Cycle:** $D = (R_1 + R_2)/(R_1 + 2R_2)$; **never** reaches exactly 50% with standard config.
- ✅ **555 Monostable Pulse Width:** $T = 1.1 RC$ — **independent of Vcc**.
- ✅ **Threshold levels:** $V_{TH} = 2V_{CC}/3$ and $V_{TL} = V_{CC}/3$ — set by internal 5 kΩ divider.
- ✅ **Pin 4 (Reset)** must be tied HIGH; **Pin 5** must have a 0.01 µF bypass capacitor to GND.
- ✅ **Discharge path** in astable: **R2 only** (Pin 7 shorts to GND when ON).
- ✅ **Op-amp astable** when R1 = R2: $f = 1/(2.2RC)$.
- ✅ **Op-amp monostable pulse width:** $T = RC \ln(1 + R_1/R_2)$.
- ✅ The 555 monostable is **non-retriggerable** during the timing cycle.
- ✅ Component tolerance (E12 = ±10%) means designed frequency deviates; use 1% metal-film or trimpot for precision.
- ✅ Always include a **0.1 µF decoupling capacitor** between Vcc (Pin 8) and GND (Pin 1), placed physically close to the IC.
- ✅ **Output current limit:** 200 mA — for heavier loads (relays, motors), use a driver stage (e.g., 2N2222 transistor or ULN2003).
- ✅ Lab observation sequence: **(i)** Build the circuit on breadboard, **(ii)** Verify Vcc = 5 V with multimeter, **(iii)** Connect CRO/DSO to Pin 3, **(iv)** Measure t_HIGH, t_LOW, and period, **(v)** Compare with theoretical calculations and report % error.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Final KTU Exam Tip:** When asked to "explain the operation", always write in this order — **(1) Circuit connection → (2) Charging phase → (3) Triggering of comparators → (4) Switching of flip-flop → (5) Discharging phase → (6) Return to original state**. This structured 6-point answer guarantees full marks in the KTU valuation scheme.
